mnemonics: Label the Image Icon (JLabel for ImageIcon) - Think of a JLabel as a picture frame that holds and displays your ImageIcon (the actual picture).
keyPoints: JLabel is a Swing component used to display a short string of text, an image, or both.
ImageIcon is a class that implements the Icon interface, used to create an icon from an image file (JPEG, GIF, PNG).
You set an ImageIcon on a JLabel using the setIcon() method to display the image.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to add the JLabel to a container (like a JPanel or JFrame) after creating it, making the image invisible.
Using an incorrect file path for the ImageIcon, resulting in a null value and no image displayed.
